name: Utah Comp Payouts for Partial Hearing Loss

mainEntity.name: How much compensation for 20% bilateral hearing loss in Utah workers' comp?
mainEntity.text: How much compensation for 20% bilateral hearing loss in Utah workers' comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah schedules hearing loss as scheduled injury; 20% binaural impairment typically yields partial lump sum based on state impairment tables, often $5,000-$15,000 depending on wages/age.

suggestedAnswer.text: Compensation uses Utah Impairment Guides for % loss x body part value. Binaural formula: % better ear x 5 + % worse = total %. Plus medical/temporary benefits. Carriers lowball; independent ratings increase payouts. Just Work Comp can maximize via appeals in Murray courts.[4]

description: Utah schedules hearing loss as scheduled injury; 20% binaural impairment typically yields partial lump sum based on state impairment tables, often $5,000-$15,00
